module.name: heatmap
module.description: A grid of numbers coloured by value (pale for low, full accent for high) with optional row/column headers, fed by rows of |-separated cells in the body.
module.aiSpec:
A HEATMAP — a grid of numeric cells shaded by value (low = pale, high = full colour). Good for correlation matrices, score grids, intensity tables. NOT for category→value bars (use @barchart) and NOT for binning raw samples (use @histogram). Block module: the body is one row per line, cells separated by ` | ` (sections split by `<!-- @ -->` are simply merged, so a single section is fine). • If the FIRST row starts with an empty first cell (`| A | B`), it is treated as a COLUMN header row. • A row whose first cell is non-numeric becomes that row's label. • Non-numeric cells render unshaded (muted text). The colour scale runs between the min and max of all numeric cells unless overridden. Params: • color — colour of the highest-value cells; lower values fade toward transparent. Default var(--accent-color); any CSS colour works. • min — value mapped to the palest shade; blank (default) = auto (data minimum). • max — value mapped to the strongest shade; blank (default) = auto (data maximum).
<!-- @heatmap --> | A | B | C A | 1 | 0.8 | 0.2 B | 0.8 | 1 | 0.5 C | 0.2 | 0.5 | 1 <!-- @end -->

param.name: color
param.label: High color
param.desc: Colour of the highest-value cells; lower values fade toward transparent.

param.name: min
param.label: Scale min
param.desc: Value mapped to the palest shade; leave blank to use the data minimum.

param.name: max
param.label: Scale max
param.desc: Value mapped to the strongest shade; leave blank to use the data maximum.

snippet.label: Heatmap grid
snippet.description: Value grid coloured low→high; optional row/column headers.
